name: Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop
name_original: Embalses de Leurtza – ruta circular hasta la cima del Txaruta
description: This moderate 15.8-mile mountain bike route circles the Embalses de Leurtza, climbing to Txaruta Summit for panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Urroz offer a diverse terrain in Navarre, northern Spain, characterized by varied landscapes suitable for different skill levels. The region features idyllic oak and beech woods, expansive mountain meadows, and rocky crags, providing a rich backdrop for mountain biking. Many routes incorporate historical pathways, including sections of the old Irati railway, adding a unique element to the rides. The area's topography includes significant elevation changes, with routes climbing over 1,000 meters, ensuring challenging options for experienced riders. Best mountain bike trails in Urroz The most popular mountain bike trail is Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route encircles the Leurtza Reservoirs and ascends to Txaruta Summit, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Bera Bridge – Lantxurda Tunnel loop from Doneztebe / Santesteban, a moderate 37.7 miles (60.7 km) path. This route incorporates sections of historical railway lines, providing a blend of natural scenery and heritage. Local mountain bikers also love the Dam and Tunnel on the River – Tunnel loop from Doneztebe / Santesteban, a 17.5 miles (28.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain featuring a dam and tunnels, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Urroz is defined by idyllic oak and beech woods, mountain meadows, and historical railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Urroz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Urroz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Urroz?
A: There are over 39 mountain bike trails around Urroz, offering a diverse range of options. These include 5 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 19 more challenging trails, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Urroz?
A: The terrain around Urroz is quite varied, featuring idyllic oak and beech woods, expansive mountain meadows, and rocky crags. Many routes also incorporate historical pathways, including sections of the old Irati railway, providing a unique blend of natural scenery and heritage. You can expect significant elevation changes on some routes, with climbs exceeding 1,000 meters.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Urroz?
A: The mountain bike trails around Urroz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the oak and beech woods, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural features to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Urroz boasts several beautiful natural features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bisusto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3604192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Putzubeltza Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oronoz and Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning forest landscapes to explore.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or interesting landmarks while mountain biking in Urroz?
A: Absolutely. The region has a rich history, and some mountain bike routes incorporate sections of the old Irati railway, blending natural beauty with historical interest. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doneztebe (Santesteban) Historic Centre&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which are nearby attractions.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families with children in Urroz?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the guide, the broader Navarre region, where Urroz is located, features 'Rail Trails' that follow disused railway lines. These are generally accessible and safe, offering a good option for families. For more information on Rail Trails in Navarre, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/rail-trails-in-navarre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Urroz dog-friendly?
A: While many outdoor areas in Navarre are generally welcoming to dogs, specific regulations for mountain bike trails can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks or protected areas, and to ensure you clean up after them. There are no explicit restrictions mentioned for the trails around Urroz, but always be mindful of local signage.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Urroz?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Urroz are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990021984/leurtza-reservoirs-txaruta-summit-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20512288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Café – Aiskolegi Trail loop from Doneztebe / Santesteban&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a moderate loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Urroz?
A: Urroz, being in northern Spain, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking from spring through autumn. The diverse regional scenery, including oak and beech woods, is particularly beautiful during these seasons. Summers are typically warm, while winters can be cooler and potentially wetter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Urroz?
A: Urroz-Villa is a municipality in Navarre, and while it's a well-connected area historically, specific public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to nearby towns like Doneztebe / Santesteban, from where some routes originate. However, having your own transport often provides the most flexibility for accessing various trail starting points.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Urroz?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, many routes start from towns or villages like Doneztebe / Santesteban. These locations typically offer public parking facilities. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking information, or look for available parking in the nearest town center.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for mountain biking in Urroz?
A: There are no specific permits mentioned for general mountain biking on the trails around Urroz. However, always respect local signage, especially when riding through private land, natural parks, or protected areas. Adhere to general trail etiquette, such as yielding to hikers and leaving no trace.
tips: Summit at 1081 masl with a demanding but entertaining and picturesque climb. The views from the geodesic landmark are wonderful with 360 degrees to enjoy the Navarrese landscape.

Leaving from the recreational area, you can completely turn it around following the path marked SL-NA 7 on a route without technical difficulty and a very picturesque landscape, always next to the reservoir.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Embalses de Leurtza mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills due to its demanding climb to the Txaruta summit and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including the tranquil Leurtza Reservoirs and lush beech forests. The highlight is the rewarding climb to the Txaruta Summit, where you can enjoy wonderful 360-degree panoramic views from its geodesic landmark.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route spans approximately 25.4 kilometers and has an estimated completion time of 2 hours and 46 minutes for mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalses de Leurtza mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the Leurtza Reservoirs, which serve as a common starting point for trails in the area. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Embalses de Leurtza mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the natural recreational area, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially around wildlife and other trail users. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Embalses de Leurtza mountain bike route. However, always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter conditions, including snow and ice, can make the route more challenging or impassable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, taking you through diverse Navarrese landscapes. You'll encounter areas with water and beech forests around the reservoirs, leading to a challenging but engaging climb towards the Txaruta summit. Expect a mix of forest paths and mountain trails.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, Leurtzako ibilbidea, and Leurtzako basoa trails, among others, offering a glimpse into the wider network of paths in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and challenging ascent, it's crucial to have good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Always carry sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate gear. Be aware of changing weather conditions, especially at higher elevations like the Txaruta Summit.
